creator: w.schwarzenfeld@utanet.at
text:
find /usr/local/include/python* -type f -print0 | xargs -0 grep PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE -A5
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h:# define _PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h-#endif
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h-#endif
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h-
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h-
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h-#if defined(__APPLE__)
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h:# define _PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h-#endif
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h-
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h:#ifdef _PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h-#ifndef __cplusplus
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h-   /* The workaround below is unsafe in C++ because
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h-    * the <locale> defines these symbols as real functions,
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h-    * with a slightly different signature.
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pyport.h-    * See issue #10910
--
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h:# define _PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h-#endif
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h-#endif
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h-
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h-
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h-#if defined(__APPLE__)
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h:# define _PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h-#endif
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h-
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h:#ifdef _PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h-#include <ctype.h>
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h-#include <wctype.h>
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h-#undef isalnum
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h-#define isalnum(c) iswalnum(btowc(c))
/usr/local/include/python3.4m/pyport.h-#undef isalpha
--
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h:# define _PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h-#endif
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h-#endif
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h-
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h-
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h-#if defined(__APPLE__)
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h:# define _PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h-#endif
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h-
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h:#ifdef _PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h-#ifndef __cplusplus
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h-   /* The workaround below is unsafe in C++ because
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h-    * the <locale> defines these symbols as real functions,
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h-    * with a slightly different signature.
/usr/local/include/python3.5m/pyport.h-    * See issue #10910
--
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h:# define _PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h-#endif
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h-#endif
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h-
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h-
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h-#if defined(__APPLE__)
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h:# define _PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h-#endif
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h-
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h:#ifdef _PY_PORT_CTYPE_UTF8_ISSUE
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h-#ifndef __cplusplus
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h-   /* The workaround below is unsafe in C++ because
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h-    * the <locale> defines these symbols as real functions,
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h-    * with a slightly different signature.
/usr/local/include/python3.6m/pyport.h-    * See issue #10910
